Petition For Order Terminating Settlement Agreement And Addendum And Entry Of Stipulated Agreement.
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Terminating Settlement Agreement And Addendum And Entry Of Stipulated Agreement.

Petition For Order Terminating Settlement Agreement And Addendum And Entry Of Stipulated Agreement.
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Continued To Oct-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Terminating Settlement Agreement And Addendum And Entry Of Stipulated Agreement.
Off Calendar Jul-13-2020 Continued To Sep-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Marchini appeared by telephone for Merced County Board of Education.

The Court stated that it is having trouble with the petition and questions how the geographical separation between Merced and Marquette University makes administration unwieldy. Mr. Marchini stated that the geographical separation between the Trustees has grown and has made administration of the trust more challenging, that getting rid of the individual trust will save about $20,000.00 a year in costs and that the coordination of joint efforts takes time. Mr. Marchini also stated that the trust purpose does not change and that each univeristy can carry out the trust purposes. The Court raised the issue of dividing the trust into sub-trusts. Mr. Marchini stated that Marquette University is still going to adhere to the trust and will carry out the terms of the trust, The Court questions how awarding is going to change and how terminating the trust will impact accomplishment of the trust purposes. Mr. Marchini requested more time to consider other alternatives. The Petition for Order Terminating Settlement Agreement, etc. is continued to September 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

Marquette Universitys Petition For Resignation Of Representative Of Trustee And To Appoint Successor Representative Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Qwalyne Lawson for (beneficiary/trustee) Marquette University.

The Court stated that this is a petition to switch representatives to handle the role as trustee, that it is ready to grant this relief and asked about the accountings that were to be filed. Ms. Lawson requested 30 days to either file the accounting or obtain a new stipulation regarding foregoing the accountings. The Court granted the petition for resignation of representative trustee and to appoint successor representative of trustee (subject to review of the order). A status was set for February 9, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the filing of an accounting or reason for not filing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Joint Petition Of Marquette University, Caroline Schuyler And David Winton For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Appointing Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-27-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kenneth Philpot for Carolyn Silver and David Winton (Trustees). Carolyn Johnston for Marquette University.

The Court stated that it received the addendum to the settlement agreement indicating that Marquette and the Mercedes school district instead of being institutional trustees would select a representative to serve as trustee of the Smith Trust. The Parties confirmed that an accounting is to be provided and that annual accountings will be filed by June 30th of each year. The Court granted the petition for order approving the settlement agreement, etc. and the addendum (subject to review of the order). Status of the accounting covering the period through June 30, 2010 was set for August 9, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The two underlying petitions are off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lazatin.

Marquette University'S Petition To Remove Trustees And To Appoint Successor Trustee, And Doe Surcharge.
Off Calendar Apr-05-2011 Continued To May-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court was advised that the Parties reached a settlement but a Petition for Approval of The Settlement Agreement has not been filed. The matter was continued to May 24, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of a Petition for Approval of The Settlement Agreement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Whitehead.

Marquette University'S Petition To Remove Trustees And To Appoint Successor Trustee, And Doe Surcharge.
Off Calendar Oct-26-2010 Continued To Dec-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ken Philpot for Respondent/Trustees. Caroline Johnson for Marquette University.

Counsel disagreed as to having Wells Fargo appointed as Trustee in the interim, but did agree to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than December 10, 2010. A status conference is set for December 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith.

Marquette University'S Petition To Remove Trustees And To Appoint Successor Trustee, And Doe Surcharge.
Off Calendar May-25-2010 Continued To Aug-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ken Philpot for Caroline Schyuler and David Winter (Trustees). Therese Cannata for Marquette University.

Mr. Philpot stated that he had the subsitution papers and that he was going to file them this morning. In addition, he stated that more formal discovery needed to be done, that depositions needed to be taken, that exchange of documents was needed, that he was working on a formal accounting, that the matter could best be disposed of through mediation or a settlement conference and that he believed that an institutional Trustee would be best. Ms. Cannata stated that there was a significant amount of dollars in the Trust, spoke of prior distributions that were made, raised issue with regard to the Trustees' fees, and stated that it would be helpful for the Court to consider appointing a temporary Trustee to evaluate how to go forward and that the accountings should continue. The Court declined appointing a temporary Trustee and suggested continual exchange of information regarding past accountings and then mediation. The matters were continued to August 24, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Continued To Apr-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Therese Cannata for Marquette University (Beneficiary). Hilary Lamar for David Winton and Caroline Schuyler (Trustees/Petitioners).

Ms. Lamar requested a continuance stating that they were trying to come to an agreement as to withdrawing the request to appoint Marianne Strotz as Successor Trustee and finding another Trustee. The Court informed Counsel that any other petitions are to be filed in this same case number. The matter was continued to April 6, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-09-2009 Continued To Dec-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Therese Cannata for Marquette University. Hilary Lamar for the Trustees.

This was a pre-granted matter. Ms. Cannata stated that there were concerns, that they opposed appointment of a new Trustee and asked the Court to appoint a temporary Trustee from the Court's list. In addition, Ms. Cannata stated that they were trying to trace assets, that land was sold in 2006 and that real property was purchased in Ohio. Ms. Lamar stated that they wanted the new Trustee appointed, that the Trustees have not withheld information, that they were making every effort to provide transparency and that Marquette did not have standing. The Court stated that the Parties were to leave things as they are for one month and requested that the Trustees take no major actions such as investing or selling property. The matter was continued to December 8, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
